description: Golden dataset: 50 policy text segments with known NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 control mappings. Control titles and statements are from the official NIST OSCAL catalog. Query text uses cleaned prose (parameter placeholders removed) for semantic search. Original verbatim statements preserved in nist_statement field.

queries.query: CA-02: Control Assessments. Select the appropriate assessor or assessment team for the type of assessment to be conducted; Develop a control assessment plan that describes the scope of the assessment including: Ensure the control assessment plan is reviewed and approved by the authorizing official or designated representative prior to conducting the assessment; Assess the controls in the system and its environment of operation to determine the extent to which the controls are implemented correctly, operating as intended, and producing the desired outcome with respect to meeting established security and privacy requirements; Produce a control assessment report that document the results of the assessment; and Provide the results of the control assessment to .

queries.query: SI-04: System Monitoring. Monitor the system to detect: Identify unauthorized use of the system through the following techniques and methods:; Invoke internal monitoring capabilities or deploy monitoring devices: Analyze detected events and anomalies; Adjust the level of system monitoring activity when there is a change in risk to organizational operations and assets, individuals, other organizations, or the Nation; Obtain legal opinion regarding system monitoring activities; and Provide to .
queries.nist_statement: Monitor the system to detect: Identify unauthorized use of the system through the following techniques and methods: {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.02 }}; Invoke internal monitoring capabilities or deploy monitoring devices: Analyze detected events and anomalies; Adjust the level of system monitoring activity when there is a change in risk to organizational operations and assets, individuals, other organizations, or the Nation; Obtain legal opinion regarding system monitoring activities; and Provide {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.03 }} to {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.04 }} {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.05 }}.

segments.text: The organization has established a formal access control policy that defines the purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, and enforcement mechanisms for controlling access to information systems and assets. This policy is reviewed annually and updated as needed.

Access control procedures are documented and distributed to all system administrators. These procedures outline step-by-step instructions for provisioning, modifying, and revoking user access across all organizational systems.

The IT department maintains a comprehensive account management process that defines allowed account types including individual, shared, group, and service accounts. Emergency and temporary accounts must be approved by the CISO and expire automatically after 72 hours.

System accounts are reviewed quarterly by account owners and the security team. Inactive accounts are disabled after 90 days of inactivity and deleted after 180 days. All account actions are logged in the centralized SIEM platform.

The system enforces role-based access control (RBAC) for all logical access to information resources. Users are granted access based on their assigned roles, and access permissions are validated at each point of entry using centralized authentication services.

Network segmentation and firewall rules enforce information flow policies between security domains. Data flowing between the production network and development environments must pass through a designated transfer gateway that inspects and logs all traffic.

Separation of duties is enforced for critical business functions. No single individual can authorize, process, and record a financial transaction. System administration and audit functions are assigned to separate personnel.

All users are provisioned with the minimum level of access required to perform their job functions. Privileged access (root, admin) is restricted to designated administrators and requires multi-factor authentication. Access requests must include documented justification.

After five consecutive failed login attempts, the user account is automatically locked for 30 minutes. The security team is notified of repeated lockouts, and persistent failed attempts trigger an investigation by the incident response team.

All organizational information systems display an approved banner before granting access. The notification warns that the system is for authorized use only, that activity may be monitored and recorded, and that unauthorized use may result in disciplinary action.

Workstations and laptops are configured to automatically lock after 15 minutes of inactivity. Users are required to authenticate with their credentials to regain access. Mobile devices lock after 5 minutes and require biometric or PIN authentication.

Remote access to organizational systems is permitted only through the approved VPN solution using multi-factor authentication. Split tunneling is disabled, and all remote sessions are logged. Remote access agreements must be signed by all telecommuting personnel.

The organization maintains a security awareness and training policy that requires all employees to complete initial security training within 30 days of hire and annual refresher training thereafter. The policy defines training requirements by role.

All employees complete annual security awareness training covering phishing identification, password hygiene, social engineering tactics, and data handling procedures. Role-based training is provided for IT staff, developers, and managers with additional modules.

The organization has documented an audit and accountability policy that defines the scope of audit logging, roles responsible for audit management, and requirements for audit log retention and protection.

The system is configured to log successful and failed authentication attempts, privilege escalation events, file access to sensitive data, configuration changes, and administrative actions. The list of auditable events is reviewed annually.

Each audit record includes the timestamp, source IP address, user identity, event type, event outcome (success or failure), and the affected resource. Records are stored in a standardized format compatible with the SIEM platform.

The security operations center reviews audit logs daily for anomalous activity. Automated correlation rules flag potential incidents for analyst review. Monthly summary reports are generated for management showing trends and notable events.
segments.control_title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting

segments.text: All servers and network devices are configured to generate audit records for organization-defined auditable events. Audit generation is enabled at the operating system, application, and database levels with centralized log forwarding to the SIEM.

The organization has documented security assessment and authorization policies that define the frequency, scope, and methodology for control assessments. Authorization packages must be reviewed and approved by the authorizing official.

Security controls are assessed annually using a combination of automated scanning tools, manual testing, and interviews with system operators. Assessment results are documented in a formal report with findings, risk ratings, and remediation timelines.

The organization employs a continuous monitoring strategy that includes automated vulnerability scanning on a weekly basis, configuration compliance checks daily, and real-time monitoring of security-relevant events through the SIEM platform.

Configuration management policies and procedures are documented and distributed to relevant personnel. The policy requires that all changes to production systems follow the formal change management process including impact assessment and approval.

A baseline configuration is documented for each system type including approved operating system versions, installed software packages, and security configuration settings. Baselines are reviewed and updated quarterly or when significant changes occur.

Systems are deployed with hardened configurations based on CIS Benchmarks. Configuration settings include disabling unnecessary services, enforcing strong cipher suites, and implementing recommended registry and kernel parameters.

The organization maintains a comprehensive inventory of all system components including hardware, software, firmware, and network devices. The inventory is updated automatically using discovery tools and reconciled manually on a quarterly basis.

Contingency planning policies define the scope, roles, and responsibilities for business continuity and disaster recovery planning. The policy requires that contingency plans be tested annually and updated following organizational or system changes.

A comprehensive contingency plan identifies essential missions and business functions, recovery objectives, and restoration priorities. The plan includes procedures for activating recovery operations and returning to normal operations.

Full system backups are performed weekly and incremental backups are performed nightly. Backup media is encrypted using AES-256 and stored at a geographically separate offsite facility. Backup restoration is tested quarterly to verify data integrity.

The organization documents and distributes identification and authentication policies that define acceptable authenticator types, password complexity requirements, and multi-factor authentication mandates for privileged and remote access.

All organizational users are uniquely identified through assigned user IDs that are not shared or reused. Authentication requires a minimum of username and password, with multi-factor authentication required for privileged access and remote sessions.

Authenticator management procedures require passwords to be at least 14 characters with complexity requirements. Passwords expire every 90 days and the last 24 passwords are remembered. Hardware tokens are inventoried and revoked upon personnel termination.

The organization maintains an incident response policy that defines reportable incidents, escalation paths, and roles and responsibilities for the incident response team. The policy is reviewed annually and tested through tabletop exercises.

The incident response team implements a structured incident handling process that includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident activity phases. All incidents are tracked through the ticketing system.

All employees are required to report suspected security incidents to the helpdesk or security team within one hour of discovery. The incident response team reports confirmed incidents to management and external authorities as required by regulation.

System maintenance policies require that all maintenance activities be scheduled, documented, and performed by authorized personnel. Remote maintenance sessions require multi-factor authentication and are monitored and logged.

Media protection policies define handling requirements for digital and non-digital media containing sensitive information. Media is classified according to the data it contains and protected with appropriate physical and logical safeguards.

All storage media is sanitized using NIST SP 800-88 approved methods before disposal or reuse. Hard drives are degaussed and physically destroyed. Sanitization activities are documented and certificates of destruction are retained.

Physical and environmental protection policies define requirements for facility access control, visitor management, environmental safeguards, and asset protection. Physical security measures are inspected annually by the facilities team.

Physical access authorization lists are maintained by the facilities security team. Authorized personnel are issued badge credentials. The authorization list is reviewed quarterly and updated within 24 hours of personnel changes.

Building entry points are controlled by badge readers and monitored by security cameras. Visitors must sign in at reception and be escorted at all times. Server rooms require two-factor authentication (badge plus biometric) for entry.

Security and privacy planning policies establish the requirements for developing, maintaining, and updating system security plans and privacy impact assessments. Plans are reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.

Risk assessment policies define the methodology, scope, and frequency of organizational risk assessments. Risk assessments are conducted annually or upon significant system changes and follow the NIST Risk Management Framework.

Vulnerability scans are conducted weekly on all networked systems using an approved scanning tool. Critical vulnerabilities must be remediated within 15 days, high within 30 days, and medium within 90 days. Scan results are reported to system owners.

System and services acquisition policies require security requirements to be included in acquisition contracts. Vendors must demonstrate compliance with organizational security standards and are subject to periodic security assessments.

System and communications protection policies define requirements for network security architecture, secure communication protocols, and boundary protection mechanisms. TLS 1.2 or higher is mandated for all data in transit.

The organization deploys firewalls, intrusion detection systems, and web application firewalls at managed network boundaries. All inbound and outbound traffic is filtered against security policies. External connections are limited to authorized services.

Cryptographic keys are generated, distributed, stored, and destroyed following documented key management procedures. Keys are stored in FIPS 140-2 validated hardware security modules. Key rotation occurs annually or upon suspected compromise.

The organization implements FIPS-validated cryptographic modules for all encryption operations. AES-256 is used for data at rest, TLS 1.3 for data in transit, and SHA-256 or stronger for hashing. Approved cipher suites are enforced system-wide.

All sensitive data stored on servers, databases, and endpoint devices is encrypted at rest using AES-256 encryption. Full disk encryption is enabled on all laptops and mobile devices. Encryption keys are managed centrally through the key management system.

System and information integrity policies require the implementation of flaw remediation, malicious code protection, and system monitoring capabilities. Integrity verification tools are deployed to detect unauthorized changes to critical files.

The organization monitors information systems using intrusion detection systems, security information and event management (SIEM) tools, and endpoint detection and response (EDR) agents. Alerts are triaged by the SOC within 15 minutes.
